visa.seoTitle: Spain Visa & Schengen Guide 2026: Requirements, Application & Tips
visa.seoDescription: Complete guide to Spain visa and Schengen requirements. Learn about visa-free entry, Schengen visa application, required documents, and tips for a smooth arrival.
visa.title: Spain Visa & Schengen Guide 2026
visa.intro: Spain is part of the Schengen Area, allowing citizens of many countries to visit visa-free for up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Here's everything you need to know about entering Spain, Schengen visa requirements, and how to apply.
visa.eVisaCardDesc: Citizens of 60+ countries can enter Spain and the Schengen Area without a visa for stays up to 90 days
visa.exemptionCardDesc: Non-exempt nationalities must apply for a Schengen visa at a Spanish consulate before travel
visa.voaCardDesc: Student, work, and digital nomad visas for stays exceeding 90 days in Spain
visa.eVisaIntro: If your country is on the Schengen visa-free list, you can visit Spain and other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within any 180-day period without a visa. Starting 2026, ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) pre-travel authorization will be required for visa-free visitors.
visa.applyStep1: Valid passport with at least 3 months validity beyond your planned stay
visa.applyStep2: ETIAS authorization (when implemented) — apply online before travel
visa.applyStep3: Proof of accommodation and return/onward ticket may be requested
visa.applyStep4: Proof of sufficient funds (approximately €100 per day) may be requested
visa.applyStep5: Travel insurance covering at least €30,000 in medical expenses recommended
visa.processingTimeDesc: ETIAS approval is typically instant, valid for 3 years or until passport expiry. Schengen visa applications should be submitted 2-6 months before travel.
visa.costDesc: ETIAS: €7 (18-70 years), free for under 18 and over 70. Schengen visa: €80 for adults, €40 for children 6-12.
visa.exemptionIntro: If your nationality requires a Schengen visa, you must apply at the Spanish consulate or visa application center in your country of residence before traveling.
visa.exemptionReq5: Proof of financial means (bank statements, employment letter)
visa.exemptionReq6: Cover letter explaining purpose of visit
visa.voaIntro: For stays exceeding 90 days, Spain offers several long-stay visa categories including student visas, work visas, the digital nomad visa (under the Startups Act), non-lucrative residence visas, and golden visas for investors.
visa.voaReq1: Student visa: acceptance letter from a Spanish educational institution
visa.voaReq2: Work visa: job offer from a Spanish employer with work permit
visa.voaReq3: Digital nomad visa: proof of remote employment with non-Spanish company, minimum income ~€28,000/year
visa.voaReq4: Non-lucrative visa: proof of passive income or savings (approximately €28,800/year)
visa.voaReq5: All types require health insurance and clean criminal record
visa.tip1: Keep a printed copy of your accommodation booking and return flight
visa.tip2: Carry proof of travel insurance — border officials may ask to see it
visa.tip3: Count your 90 days carefully if visiting multiple Schengen countries
visa.tip4: Spanish border officials are generally efficient but may ask about your purpose of visit
visa.tip5: EU citizens can use e-gates at major airports for faster processing
visa.tip6: Register with local police (empadronamiento) if staying more than 3 months

weather.seoTitle: Spain Weather Guide 2026: Monthly Climate, Best Time to Visit
weather.seoDescription: Complete Spain weather guide with monthly temperatures, rainfall data, and regional climate information. Plan the perfect time for your Spanish adventure.
weather.title: Spain Weather Guide 2026
weather.intro: Spain's diverse geography creates multiple climate zones, from the rainy Green Coast in the north to the arid Mediterranean south and subtropical Canary Islands. Understanding regional weather patterns is key to planning your trip.
weather.monthlyDesc: Average temperatures and conditions across Spain's main regions throughout the year
weather.bestTimeDesc: Spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) offer the best weather for sightseeing, with comfortable temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is ideal for beaches and northern Spain. Winter is perfect for the Canary Islands and southern coast.
weather.whatToPackDesc: Light layers for spring and autumn, sun protection year-round, rain gear for northern Spain, and warm layers for winter evenings in inland cities.
weather.mediterraneanCoast: Mediterranean Coast: Hot, dry summers and mild winters. Barcelona, Valencia, and the Costa del Sol.
weather.centralPlateau: Central Plateau (Meseta): Continental climate with hot summers and cold winters. Madrid, Toledo, Salamanca.
weather.northernCoast: Northern Coast (Green Spain): Oceanic climate with mild temperatures and frequent rain. Basque Country, Galicia, Asturias.
weather.canaryIslands: Canary Islands: Subtropical, warm year-round (20-28°C). Ideal winter escape.
weather.andalusia: Andalusia Interior: Very hot summers (40°C+), mild winters. Seville, Cordoba, Granada.

transport.seoTitle: Spain Transport Guide 2026: Trains, Buses, Flights & Getting Around
transport.seoDescription: Complete guide to transportation in Spain. Navigate Renfe trains, AVE high-speed rail, buses, domestic flights, and city transport systems.
transport.title: Spain Transport Guide 2026
transport.intro: Spain has excellent transportation infrastructure, from one of Europe's best high-speed rail networks to affordable domestic flights and extensive bus services. Here's how to get around.
transport.trainsDesc: Spain's high-speed AVE network connects major cities in hours. Madrid to Barcelona takes 2.5 hours, Madrid to Seville 2.5 hours. Budget alternatives include AVLO, Ouigo, and Iryo. Regional and commuter trains (Cercanias) serve metropolitan areas.
transport.busesDesc: Long-distance buses are the most affordable option and reach destinations trains don't. ALSA is the main operator with extensive coverage. FlixBus offers competitive pricing on popular routes.
transport.flightsDesc: Budget airlines like Vueling, Ryanair, and Iberia Express connect Spanish cities cheaply, especially to the islands. Book in advance for fares from €15-30 one-way.
transport.metroDesc: Madrid and Barcelona have extensive metro systems. Valencia, Seville, Bilbao, and Malaga have metro or tram networks. Multi-ride cards offer the best value.
transport.carRentalDesc: Essential for rural exploration, road trips through Andalusia, and island hopping. Major international companies and local operators available at airports. Note: tolls apply on some highways (autopistas).
transport.ferriesDesc: Regular ferry services connect mainland Spain with the Balearic Islands (Balearia, Trasmediterranea) and between Canary Islands (Fred Olsen, Naviera Armas). Also connects to Morocco and Italy.

food.seoTitle: Spanish Food Guide 2026: Traditional Dishes, Tapas & Regional Cuisine
food.seoDescription: Discover authentic Spanish cuisine with our comprehensive food guide. From tapas and paella to regional specialties, learn about Spain's incredible culinary traditions.
food.title: Spanish Food Guide 2026
food.intro: Spanish cuisine is one of the world's great culinary traditions, built on fresh Mediterranean ingredients, bold flavors, and centuries of regional diversity. From Basque Country pintxos to Andalusian tapas, every region has its own distinctive dishes.

drinks.seoTitle: Spanish Drinks Guide 2026: Wine, Sangria, Sherry & More
drinks.seoDescription: Explore Spanish beverages from world-famous Rioja wines and Jerez sherry to refreshing sangria and horchata. Your complete guide to drinks in Spain.
drinks.title: Spanish Drinks Guide 2026
drinks.intro: Spain's drink culture is as rich and varied as its cuisine. From world-renowned wine regions producing Rioja, Ribera del Duero, and Priorat to the unique sherries of Jerez and refreshing summer drinks like tinto de verano, there's always something new to taste.

islands.seoTitle: Spanish Islands Guide 2026: Balearics, Canaries & More
islands.seoDescription: Complete guide to Spain's beautiful islands. Explore the Balearic Islands (Mallorca, Menorca, Ibiza) and Canary Islands (Tenerife, Gran Canaria, Lanzarote).
islands.title: Spanish Islands Guide 2026
islands.intro: Spain's islands offer two distinct paradises. The Balearic Islands in the Mediterranean deliver stunning coves, glamorous nightlife, and Mediterranean charm. The Canary Islands off Africa's coast provide year-round sunshine, volcanic landscapes, and diverse outdoor adventures.

regions.seoTitle: Spain Regions Guide 2026: Explore Every Corner of Spain
regions.seoDescription: Discover Spain's diverse regions from Andalusia to the Basque Country. Each region offers unique culture, cuisine, landscapes, and experiences.
regions.title: Spanish Regions Guide 2026
regions.intro: Spain's 17 autonomous communities each have their own distinct identity, cuisine, and traditions. From the flamenco and Moorish architecture of Andalusia to the Gaudi masterpieces of Catalonia and the culinary excellence of the Basque Country, exploring Spain's regions reveals the country's incredible diversity.

practicalInfo.seoTitle: Spain Practical Travel Info 2026: Tips, Safety, Money & More
practicalInfo.seoDescription: Essential practical information for visiting Spain. Currency, tipping, safety tips, emergency numbers, electricity, language, and cultural customs.
practicalInfo.title: Spain Practical Travel Info 2026
practicalInfo.intro: Everything you need to know for a smooth trip to Spain, from currency and tipping to safety, communications, and cultural norms.

esim.seoTitle: Best eSIM for Spain 2026: Stay Connected While Traveling
esim.seoDescription: Compare the best eSIM options for traveling in Spain. Get affordable mobile data with Airalo, Saily, and other providers. Coverage, prices, and setup guide.
esim.title: Best eSIM for Spain 2026
esim.intro: Stay connected during your Spain trip with an eSIM. Modern eSIMs offer affordable data plans without the hassle of physical SIM cards. Here's how to choose the best option for your needs.

insurance.seoTitle: Travel Insurance for Spain 2026: Best Plans & Coverage Guide
insurance.seoDescription: Find the best travel insurance for your Spain trip. Compare coverage for medical emergencies, trip cancellation, baggage loss, and adventure activities.
insurance.title: Travel Insurance for Spain 2026
insurance.intro: While Spain has excellent healthcare, travel insurance is essential for covering unexpected medical costs, trip cancellations, lost baggage, and adventure activities. EU citizens should carry a European Health Insurance Card (EHIC), but comprehensive travel insurance is still recommended.
